News.title: Foreign Office warns British tourists on Cyprus to do 5 things now - see the full list
News.content:
Foreign Office warns British tourists on Cyprus to do 5 things now - see the full list

RAF Akrotiri on Cyprus was hit by a drone, which has threatened the safety of British military personnel, and now the Foreign Office has issued a warning to tourists there

View 3 Images Tourists stand on a beach in Varosha in Famagusta, Cyprus (Image: Getty Images)

British tourists on Cyprus should follow five key steps to ensure their safety, the Foreign Office says.

‌



Visitors on the island in the Mediterranean Sea have been ordered to keep their departure plans under review and ensure their travel documents are up to date amid the conflict in the Middle East. RAF Akrotiri on Cyprus was hit by a drone on Monday, after which 60 flights to and from Cypriot airports were cancelled.

‌



The UK will send HMS Dragon, one of the Royal Navy's six Type 45 air defence destroyers, but tourists should also take action. The Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO)'s website reads: "Regional escalation poses significant security risks and has led to travel disruption."

‌



Iran war LIVE as Ali Khamenei's son Mojtaba named as new Supreme Leader READ MORE :

View 3 Images A Fighter Jet takes off from the UK's RAF Akrotiri airbase (Image: AP)

As Birmingham Live reports, the FCDO's full five tips are;

read guidance on “how to prepare for a crisis” on the FCDO website, with suggestions on what you might include in your emergency supplies and “what to do in a crisis”

‌



sign up to FCDO Travel Advice email alerts

monitor local and international media

sign up to local information alerts/resources AND follow the advice of local authorities

‌



keep your departure plans under review AND ensure your travel documents are up to date

The conflict and tension in the Middle East and Gulf states continues. Donald Trump, though, has said he is "not happy" with the UK after it did not initially allow the US to use its bases for strikes on Iran, and added that Keir Starmer is "not Winston Churchill".

It is the latest in a series of broadsides from the US president over the UK's position on the Middle East war. Meanwhile, the Foreign Secretary confirmed that a Government charter flight will take off from Muscat, Oman, "in the coming days" to bring vulnerable British nationals back.

‌



View 3 Images HMS Dragon is one of the Royal Navy's six Type 45 air defence destroyers (Image: Crown copyright)

The HMS Dragon, sent from Portsmouth in Hampshire and crewed by more than 200 people, is designed for air defence, it is equipped with a Sea Viper anti-air missile system. Defence Secretary John Healey said: "We are moving quickly to further reinforce our defensive presence in the Eastern Mediterranean.

"HMS Dragon brings world-class air defence capability, and our Wildcat helicopters are armed with Martlet missiles to counter the growing drone threat.

Article continues below

"I am deeply proud of the professionalism and bravery of our Armed Forces personnel who have in recent days, successfully taken action across the region to protect our allies and defend British interests."

Dozens of countries remain on a list, compiled by FCDO, which Brits should not visit due to the ongoing conflict. These include holiday hotspots, such as Egypt and the United Arab Emirates.

News.title: Facebook down LIVE as worldwide outage impacts UK and US
News.content:
Facebook is down with widespread reports of people being unable to log in to the social media platform.

The DownDetector website shows a massive spike in Facebook users reporting problems accessing the site in both the UK and US at around 9.40pm.

By 9:47pm there had been more than 1,600 reports of trouble getting onto Facebook in the UK.

News.title: Andrew 'gives up £13,000-a-year Crown Estate house' MONTHS after being booted out of Royal Lodge
News.content:
Andrew 'gives up £13,000-a-year Crown Estate house' MONTHS after being booted out of Royal Lodge

The former Prince Andrew is said to have been paying just over £13,000 per year to rent the property - which is believed to have been used to house staff close to his previous home

View 4 Images Prince Andrew is said to still be renting the East Lodge (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor has reportedly given up his lease on yet another Crown Estate property after he was kicked off Royal Lodge last year.

The former Duke of York is said to have asked to end his lease on the East Lodge, a £13,000-a-year Grade II-listed cottage rental near Sunninghill Park in Berkshire. The property, located near his former home in Sunninghill Park, Berkshire, is the second within the Crown Estate to have been given up by the King's brother after he vacated Royal Lodge in late 2025.

The move to drop the property comes nearly 30 years after the royal took on the tenancy, and as mystery swirls about how he was able to pay for it.

Netflix in talks to revive The Crown for special on Andrew’s arrest READ MORE :

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

View 4 Images East Lodge cost the former Prince around £13,500 per year (Image: Google Maps)

The BBC reports that lease documents show the then-Duke had started his tenancy at East Lodge in February 1998, initially paying up to £3,500 annually for the Crown Estate Property. According to the same documents seen by the national broadcaster, that rent has shot up by nearly £10,000 over the following nearly 30 years.

The property was located near his much larger former personal home in Sunninghill Park, Berkshire, and was believed to have been used for staff accommodation.

He had reportedly kept his tenancy of the property in an arrangement with the Crown Estate, meaning it stayed in his hands months past his residency of Royal Lodge, which he vacated late last year. The new documents will raise questions as to how he was able to pay for East Lodge, rent for which cost more than half his declared annual income.

View 4 Images Andrew vacated Royal Lodge in late 2025 (Image: Getty Images)

Andrew was all but expelled from his family following continued outrage stemming from his association with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, with his move from Royal Lodge in late 2025 sparked by continued revelations.

He has always denied any wrongdoing stemming from his relationship with the sex trafficker, but the association saw King Charles III take away his £1million annual royal allowance in 2024. He was left with his £20,000 declared annual Naval pension to pay for the 30-bedroom mansion and estimated £3million-a-year security bill.

A Royal Lodge lease released in October 2025 showed he paid a "one peppercorn" of rent on the mansion for more than 20 years after signing a 75-year lease and paying £1 million in 2004.

Article continues below

View 4 Images The property is located near the former Duke of York's previous personal home (Image: Tim Graham/Getty Images)

According to the documents seen by the BBC, there was a provision on rent for East Lodge that saw rent increase with inflation, rising to £8,047 per year by the time it was renewed in 2020. Documents from late August 2025 showed the rent had risen to £12,922 in an area where rental prices range from around £2,000 to £7,500 per month.

Andrew's tenancy was set to end in July 2027, but is set to end early following BBC probing of the property, the broadcaster said. The Crown Estate said in a statement: "Since then… we have received a request for us to consider an early termination of the lease."

News.title: Armed police swoop on Oldham Asda as 'aggressive man with weapon makes threats'
News.content:
Armed police swooped on an incident near an Asda store, after reports of an “aggressive male” feared to be armed with a knife.

Pictures showed armed police on Littlemoor Lane in Oldham following reports of a concerning incident close to the supermarket. Cops were called after a member of the public reported the man for allegedly making threats outside the store.

The caller reported seeing a handle which may have been part of a knife, prompting armed police to be called to the scene shortly before 1pm, the Manchester Evening News reports.

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

Picture from Littlemoor Lane showed a number of police vehicles and a number of armed and unarmed police, who appeared to be concentrating on one address in the road.

No arrests have been made and no weapons have been seized.

A spokesman for Greater Manchester Police said: "At around 12.45pm on Tuesday 3 March 2026, we were called to reports of an aggressive male on Huddersfield Road in Oldham.

"Officers attended the scene and established that a male made verbal threats before making off. No arrests have been made at this stage, and investigations are ongoing."

News.title: UK facing urgent Iran terror warning and 'lone-wolf' attacks over Middle East war
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: UK facing urgent Iran terror warning and 'lone-wolf' attacks over Middle East war

'Stuck between a rock and a hard place' nightmare for the UK as it faces a worrying choice between infuriating Donald Trump by not backing his Iran war - or raising the UK terror threat

View 9 Images US-Israeli war on Iran spreads to Beirut's southern suburbs (Image: Xinhua/Shutterstock)

Britain could face terror attacks from pro-Iranian terror networks and lone-wolf sympathisers, an Israeli intelligence expert has warned.

‌



Ex-Israeli forces intelligence officer Danny Citrinowicz said the UK is stuck between alienating President Trump by not getting involved - and suffering a greater terror threat. It came after the UK confirmed it would send a warship to the Middle East to protect UK interests.

‌



Asked if there was a risk to the UK, Citrinowicz said the UK faces terror threats from the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and also Tehran regime sympathisers. He said: “Definitely the risk is there. And the risk will increase if the UK joins the war. And that is obvious.

‌



Keir Starmer deploys warship HMS Dragon to Cyprus as Iran crisis spirals READ MORE :

Former Defence Secretary Ben Wallace installed early favourite for vacant BHA role READ MORE :

View 9 Images Protests spread to Kashmir among Khamenei supporters (Image: Firdous Nazir/NurPhoto/Shutterstock)

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

“You have the risk from the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, who are in the UK and also from the elements living in the UK. They may feel bound to do something because of the so called decapitation of Khamenei. Definitely the risk is there and it will increase by joining the war.”

‌



Mr Citrinowicz, who is now senior researcher in the Institute for National Security Studies in Tel Aviv, added: “But the UK has to navigate, between that risk and the risk of President Trump taking action because you didn’t join the war.” But he added that apart from the threat of IRGC sponsored terror: “..more importantly, much more importantly is the lone wolf phenomenon, especially from people living in the UK.” It has long been suspected pro-Iranian regime sleeper cells loyal to or handled by the IRGC were in the UK and could launch attacks if called upon.

View 9 Images Destroyer Hms Duncan may deploy to region (Image: AFP/Getty Images)

Asked to specify that he means an actual terror attack he replied: “Yes, definitely.” The US-Israeli war on Iran has exploded further around the Middle East with major strikes on Iran and Lebanon - as the UK appeared to become further involved in the crisis.

‌



A £650m British warship will be deployed to the region to protect the UK military base at RAF Akrotiri, in Cyprus, from drone attacks launched by Iran or its proxies. The Type 45 destroyer HMS Dragon will be sent to the region, its crew having recently completed a training exercise on taking down drones. It comes after UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer green-lighted the US using British bases for "defensive" strikes on Iran's missile sites, after US-Israeli strikes started.

View 9 Images Saudi Ras Tanura oil refinery attacked (Image: Satellite image Â©2026 Vantor/AFP)

A drone on Sunday hit RAF Akrotiri, a British sovereign air base on the island, though the Prime Minister said it was launched before his announcement and was not in retaliation. Shadow defence secretary James Cartlidge said HMS Dragon should "go now" because "we need the highly capable air defence it provides to protect RAF Akrotiri".

‌



He added: "It begs the question: why wasn't she already there?" Foreign secretary Yvette Cooper said the government had already added to the air defences in Cyprus, adding: "We have already increased the deployment, with additional defensive capabilities, including radar, F-35 jets, air defence, and counter-drone systems.”

View 9 Images Head to head: Donald Trump and Keir Starmer (Image: AP)

It comes as US Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned the “hardest hits” on Iran are “yet to come,” and amid concern US President Donald Trump’s war could last a long time. On Monday he claimed it may go on for ‘four to five weeks” but that is believed now to be an underestimate, despite sources saying Washington originally thought strikes would last days.

‌



It now appears the conflict, triggered by US President Donald Trump’s attack on Iran, is widening by the hour, with rising death tolls and fears of a long-term war. The US warned of an imminent attack in the Saudi City of Dharan, a major centre for the oil industry, as Tehran attempts to lash out further at the economy of the Gulf states.

View 9 Images Man shouts anti-US slogans in Tehran (Image: Morteza Nikoubazl/NurPhoto/Shutterstock)

And it has closed down its embassies in Saudi Arabia and Kuwait after drone attacks including one on its embassy in Riyadh as Iran retaliates across the Middle East. Saudi Arabia’s defence ministry confirmed the US embassy in Riyadh was hit by two drones, which sparked a fire and minor material damage to the building.

‌



A loud blast was heard and flames seen at the embassy early on Tuesday morning and black smoke was seen rising over Riyadh’s diplomatic quarter, which houses foreign missions. Tehran has already claimed to have closed down the key Hormuz Strait through which much of the world’s oil and gas supplies transit, saying it will “set on fire” any ships trying to pass. And President Donald Trump told Iran it's “too late” to talk and that its "air defense, air force, navy, and leadership is gone".

View 9 Images Iran attack: US Super Hornet takes off from USS Lincoln (Image: US Navy / SWNS)

Israel has yet again launched ground troops into southern Lebanon, warning locals to flee the region as it stepped up attacks on Iran’s proxy network Hezbollah. As well as major strikes on Tehran, including blasting the Presidential office, and throughout Iran Beirut was hit as Israeli warplanes demolished a key Hezbollah HQ.

‌



Death tolls throughout the region continued to pile up with the worst in Iran as 787 people are known to have been killed, including 176 children. The number of US service members killed is now six and deaths in Beirut come to 50, whilst there have also been casualties reported in the Gulf States.

View 9 Images Under attack: RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus (Image: Xinhua/Shutterstock)

The US has ordered all Americans to leave the Middle East in a cross-region alert as tens of thousands of Brits remain stranded on the fourth day of the war. The families of UK service personnel in Cyprus have been moved away from the base as a precaution against Iranian attack.

‌



On Monday, a spokesperson for the Cypriot government said two further unmanned drones heading towards the base had been intercepted. Mr Healey said the drone that struck Akrotiri was an example of the "dangerous and indiscriminate attacks" by Iran and its proxies.

View 9 Images Fighter jet over RAF Akrotiri as war spreads (Image: Xinhua/Shutterstock)

Cypriot government spokesman Konstantinos Letymbiotis spoke about the possibility of French support on Monday, telling reporters it could be "support similar to that of Greece".

Article continues below

French President Emmanuel Macron and Cypriot President Nikos Christodoulides had a phone call "in the context of strengthening the precautionary measures being taken", he said.

News.title: US commander tells troops Trump was 'chosen by Jesus to unleash Iran Armageddon'
News.content:
US commander tells troops Trump was 'chosen by Jesus to unleash Iran Armageddon'

A shocking report claims a US combat-unit commander claimed Donald Trump was 'anointed by Jesus to light the signal fire in Iran to cause Armageddon' after Saturday's attack

View 3 Images Trump speaking at the White House today (Image: SAMUEL CORUM/POOL/EPA/Shutterstock)

US troops have reportedly been told by commanders that Donald Trump was "anointed by Jesus" to attack Iran and "cause Armageddon".

‌



Trump announced Saturday that the US and Israel launched a joint operation and struck Iran, killing Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The attack was launched following Iranian nuclear negotiations and weeks of civil unrest in Iran.

‌



After the initial attack, Trump said "heavy and pinpoint bombing" would continue "uninterrupted throughout the week or, as long as necessary to achieve our objective of PEACE THROUGHOUT THE MIDDLE EAST AND, INDEED, THE WORLD!"

‌



The operation comes less than a year after Trump ordered strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities in June 2025, during which the President claimed to have obliterated Iran's nuclear capabilities. Trump told The New York Times this week that he ordered US troops to continue attacking Iran for "four to five weeks", without providing details of his plan.

View 3 Images A person stands on the roof of a building looking at a plume of smoke rises after a strike on the Iranian capital Tehran, on March 3 (Image: ATTA KENARE, AFP via Getty Images)

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

‌



A fresh report found that hundreds of US troops across dozens of units and installations have submitted complaints to non-profit watchdog Military Religious Freedom Foundation (MRFF) since the attack, about combat unit commanders providing Christian reasoning for the war, independent journalist Jonathan Larsen reports on Substack.

Non-commissioned officers who attended a briefing Monday told the MRFF that a combat-unit commander "urged us to tell our troops that this was ‘all part of God’s divine plan’ and he specifically referenced numerous citations out of the Book of Revelation referring to Armageddon and the imminent return of Jesus Christ", Mirror US reports

The commander also reportedly argued that Trump was "anointed by Jesus to light the signal fire in Iran to cause Armageddon and mark his return to Earth" and that the Iran war is part of God's plan.

‌



View 3 Images The aftermath of Israeli and US airstrikes is seen at Nilufar Square (Image: Anadolu via Getty Images)

The remarks were submitted in more than 110 complaints about commanders across all military branches, including more than 40 units across 30 military installations, the MFRR told Larsen.

At least one complainant from an NCO identified themselves as a Christian, who could be deployed at any time to Iran, writing on behalf of 15 troops, including 11 Christians, one Muslim and one Jew.

Article continues below

In the NCO's Monday email, he warned that their commander’s remarks would "destroy morale and unit cohesion and are in violation of the oaths we swore to support the [C]onstitution".

MRFF President Mikey Weinstein, an Air Force veteran, told Larsen that his office has been “inundated” with such complaints, explaining: "These calls have one damn thing in freaking common; our MRFF clients [service members who seek MRFF aid] report the unrestricted euphoria of their commanders and command chains as to how this new 'biblically-sanctioned' war is clearly the undeniable sign of the expeditious approach of the fundamentalist Christian 'End Times' as vividly described in the New Testament Book of Revelation."

He added: "Many of their commanders are especially delighted with how graphic this battle will be zeroing in on how bloody all of this must become in order to fulfill and be in 100% accordance with fundamentalist Christian end of the world eschatology."
